answer: The capital of France is Paris. It is the largest city and a global center for fashion and culture. Paris has been the capital since 508 AD.

results.title: Paris facts: the capital of France in history
results.snippet: ## Paris facts: Paris, the **capital of France**. Paris is the **capital of France**, the largest country of Europe with 550 000 km2 (65 millions inhabitants). Paris has 2.234 million inhabitants end 2011. ## Paris facts: Paris history. From the Romans to the Vth Republic, Paris has a rich 2000 year history. See details of Paris history. ## Paris facts: Paris, a world city. Paris is a world capital city of shopping and fashion, with Channel, Dior, Vuitton, Yves Saint Laurent among many other top french fashion brands. ## Paris facts: the capital of France in history. Before Paris, the capital of France was Lyon (under the Romans). Paris first became the capital of France in 508 under King Clovis. After centuries with no unique capital of France, Paris retrieved its status of capital of France under King Philippe Auguste, who reigned between 1180 and 1223. Paris remained the capital of France until today, with one four year interruption.

results.title: List of capitals of France - Wikipedia
results.snippet: * Paris (987–1419), the residence of the Kings of France, although they were consecrated at Reims. * Troyes (1419–1422), for a short time during the Hundred Years' War, the city was the seat of the royal government. * Paris (1594–1682)*[citation needed*]. * Bordeaux (September 1914), the government was relocated from Paris to Bordeaux very briefly during World War I, when it was feared that Paris would soon fall into German hands. * Tours (10–13 June 1940), the city served as the temporary capital of France during World War II after the government fled Paris due to the German advance. * Bordeaux (June 1940), the government was relocated from Paris to Tours then Bordeaux very briefly during World War II, when it became apparent that Paris would soon fall into German hands. * Brazzaville (1940–1943), with metropolitan France under Axis powers rule, Brazzaville was announced as the seat of the Free France government. * Algiers (1943–1944), the city was made the seat of Free France, to be closer to the war in Europe.

results.title: Paris 🇫🇷, the capital and largest city of France, is located in ...
results.snippet: Paris , the capital and largest city of France, is located in the northern part of the country on the banks of the Seine River . Including the surrounding metropolitan area, the population of Greater Paris reaches around 13 million people . Paris traces its roots back to the 3rd century BC when it was founded by the Celtic Parisii tribe on an island in the Seine River. During the Middle Ages , Paris grew into a significant religious and commercial center, witnessing the construction of Notre-Dame Cathedral in the 12th century and the founding of the University of Paris (Sorbonne) in the 13th century. In the 19th century, the city underwent major transformations under the supervision of Baron Haussmann , who redesigned its streets and infrastructure, giving Paris its modern architectural character. results.title: Facts About France | Map, Population & Capital - Study.com
results.snippet: # Facts About France | Map, Population & Capital. ## The Country of France. France has been a democracy since Napoleon III was overthrown in 1870, except for a brief interlude when it was occupied by Nazi Germany during the Second World War. To unlock this lesson you must be a Study.com Member. ## Where is France? ### Map of France. ## Facts About France. Thanks to France's cultural influence and extensive colonial empire, many other countries also have large French-speaking populations. ### France Population. ### Capital of France. Paris is the capital of France and one of the most well-known cities in the world. #### What are five interesting facts about France? France's capital is Paris. France has a population of 68 million. The language of France (French) is spoken by many more millions of people across the world. #### *Facts About France | Map, Population & Capital* Related Study Materials. * Landmarks in France Lesson for Kids.
